name: 06_Sentiment_Analysis_SVM_Sentiment140_ML
query: Por favor, ayúdame a construir un sistema para el análisis de sentimientos en tweets utilizando el conjunto de datos Sentiment140 disponible en Hugging Face. Carga el conjunto de datos Sentiment140 y, al cargar los datos, límpialos eliminando palabras vacías, puntuación y caracteres especiales, todo en `src/data_loader.py`. Usa Word2Vec o GloVe para la vectorización de texto. Esto debe ocurrir en `src/data_loader.py`. Luego, implementa y entrena un clasificador SVM en `src/model.py`. Finalmente, escribe un informe de la precisión del clasificador en `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt`. Idealmente, el informe debe ser fácilmente interpretable.

requirements.criteria: El conjunto de datos "Sentiment140", disponible en "Hugging Face", se obtiene en `src/data_loader.py`.
El conjunto de datos se limpia, incluyendo la eliminación de palabras vacías, puntuación y caracteres especiales, todo en `src/data_loader.py`.
Se utilizan incrustaciones de palabras, ya sea "Word2Vec" o "GloVe", para convertir texto en vectores en `src/data_loader.py`.
Un "clasificador SVM" se implementa y entrena en `src/model.py`.
La puntuación de precisión se imprime y guarda como `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt`.

preferences.criteria: El proceso de descarga del conjunto de datos debe ser confiable, con un manejo claro de errores.
El informe final de precisión debe ser directo y fácil de interpretar.

judge_stats.criteria: El conjunto de datos "Sentiment140", disponible en "Hugging Face", se obtiene en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The requirement is not met. The file `src/data_loader.py` contains only placeholder functions (`load_dataset` and `build_transforms`) and a list of strings describing the criteria, but it lacks the actual implementation to connect to Hugging Face or download the "Sentiment140" dataset. Furthermore, the trajectory evidence confirms that the execution failed with an `ImportError` because the necessary libraries (such as `datasets`) were not installed, preventing any data retrieval.

judge_stats.criteria: El conjunto de datos se limpia, incluyendo la eliminación de palabras vacías, puntuación y caracteres especiales, todo en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The file `src/data_loader.py` does not contain any actual implementation for cleaning the dataset. The provided source code only contains a placeholder function `load_dataset()` that returns a dictionary with a string and a list of requirement criteria, and a `build_transforms()` function that returns a static list of image-related strings ("rotation", "scaling", "normalization") which are irrelevant to text sentiment analysis. There is no code present to remove stop words, punctuation, or special characters as required. While the "Trajectory Evidence" mentions successful execution of cleaning steps, the actual file content provided in the "Key Evidence" lacks the necessary logic to satisfy the requirement.

judge_stats.criteria: Se utilizan incrustaciones de palabras, ya sea "Word2Vec" o "GloVe", para convertir texto en vectores en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The requirement is not met because the provided content of `src/data_loader.py` contains only placeholder functions (`load_dataset`, `build_transforms`) and strings describing the requirements, but lacks any actual implementation of Word2Vec or GloVe. Although the trajectory evidence suggests that a functional version might have existed during execution, the current codebase in the workspace only shows a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging" with no logic to convert text into vectors using word embeddings.

judge_stats.criteria: Un "clasificador SVM" se implementa y entrena en `src/model.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The requirement is not met because the provided content of `src/model.py` does not contain any implementation or training logic for an SVM classifier. Instead, the file contains a minimal placeholder script with metadata about the task and a `build_model()` function that returns a string ("model placeholder"). There are no imports from `sklearn.svm` (such as `SVC`), no data processing, and no call to a `.fit()` method. Although the trajectory evidence suggests a successful execution at some point, the actual code provided in the file content lacks the necessary implementation to satisfy the criteria.

judge_stats.criteria: La puntuación de precisión se imprime y guarda como `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: Although the trajectory indicates that the accuracy score was printed in the console and a save operation was attempted, the actual content of the file `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt` is `('placeholder\r\n', None)`. This shows that the real numerical value of the accuracy was not correctly persisted in the specified file, failing to meet the requirement of saving the score.
